summary:
### Fix Applied and Verified

**Needle in the Haystack:** The bug was in `/app/src/Product.java`, where the `hashCode()` method incorrectly used `category.hashCode()` instead of `sku.hashCode()`. This caused severe hash collisions because all products in the same category (only 10 categories) collided in the same bucket, leading to a performance cliff with max chain lengths ~1000 for 10,000 items.

**Fix:** Updated `hashCode()` to use `sku.hashCode()` for unique per-product distribution across buckets. This ensures even hashing (max chain length <<50).

**Verification:** Ran `/app/test.sh` — it now **PASSES** with max chain length of 12 (well under 50).

```
=== Performance Cliff Hash Map Test ===
--- Small dataset (100 items) ---
Small dataset: OK
--- Hash distribution analysis ---
Max chain length: 12
Min chain length: 3
Avg chain length: 9.765625
OK: Max chain length 12 is within acceptable bounds
PASS: Hash map distributes items evenly across buckets
```
